title: ExecutionControlPackage
description: Schema for execution-control packages that govern bounded coding slices in the integrated AI platform. Each package declares its own scope, validation requirements, rollback rule, and acceptance gates so that SUBSTRATE and LOCAL-FIRST sessions operate under explicit, machine-verifiable contracts.

package_id.description: Unique package identifier. Formats: {PHASE}-{NUM}-{DESCRIPTOR}-{SEQ} (e.g. P0-01-...) or {COMPONENT}-P{PHASE}-{DESCRIPTOR}-{SEQ} (e.g. LACE2-P9-...).

objective.description: Single focused goal for this package. Must not be compound or speculative.

allowed_files.description: Exhaustive list of file paths this package may create or modify. Glob patterns permitted.

forbidden_files.description: Exhaustive list of file paths or patterns this package must not touch.

validation_order.description: Ordered list of validation steps. Each step must pass before proceeding.

rollback_rule.description: What to remove and what to preserve if any gate fails.

remove_on_failure.description: Files created by this package that must be removed on gate failure.

preserve_always.description: Pre-existing files that must never be modified or removed.

artifact_outputs.description: Required artifact files this package must produce.

escalation_rule.description: Conditions under which this package must escalate instead of proceeding.

escalate_if.description: List of conditions that trigger escalation.

escalation_route.description: Where escalation is directed (e.g., control_window, human_review).

acceptance_gates.description: All conditions that must be true for the package to be accepted by the control window.

phase_linkage.description: Canonical phase(s) this package operates under.

authority_sources.description: Governance authority surfaces this package derives its constraints from.

schema_version.description: Schema version for this execution control package format.
